name: Seoul (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the bright future of the city
the sun, vitality, and Seoulites' passion
the Han River and the sky, evoking openness and progress
the mountains around the city and harmony with nature

description: A white field charged at the centre with the 'I·SEOUL·U' city brand emblem in stylised lettering, with a red dot above 'I', a blue dot above the U, and accent strokes over the central SEOUL.
history: Seoul, capital of South Korea and a Special City (Teukbyeolsi), has used several official symbols since its formal designation as the capital of the Republic of Korea in 1948. The current city flag bears the 'I·SEOUL·U' brand mark adopted on 28 October 2015, replacing the previous 'Hi Seoul' brand of 2002. The mark uses the colours of the Taegeuk and was selected by public competition to express that 'You and I together make Seoul'.

features.symbolism: the city brand 'I·SEOUL·U' (read 'I and you, together as Seoul') in stylised lettering with a red dot over 'I' (yang) and a blue dot over 'U' (eum), echoing the Taegeuk of the national flag and expressing the union of citizens and city
